MatchState.title: MatchState
MatchState.description: Represents the overall state of a match, including scores, timer, events, and team names.

scores.description: The match score; number of goals scored for each team. (Relationship: MatchState 1:1 Score)

fouls.description: The number of fouls commited for each team. (Relationship: MatchState 1:1 Fouls)

events.description: List of match events, like goals, cards, fouls, and notes. (Relationship: MatchState 1:N Event)

teamNames.description: Names of the teams. (Relationship: MatchState 1:1 TeamNames)

Score.title: Score
Score.description: Represents the score of the match

Fouls.title: Fouls
Fouls.description: Represents the number of fouls committed for each team.

home.description: Number of fouls committed by the home team.

away.description: Number of fouls committed by the away team.

Timer.title: Timer
Timer.description: Represents the state of the game timer.

isRunning.description: Indicates whether the timer is currently running.

totalPausedSeconds.description: Total number of seconds the timer has been paused.

startTime.description: Timestamp of the last time the timer was started or resumed.

period.description: Current period of the match (e.g., PRE_MATCH, P1, HALF_TIME, P2, FULL_TIME).

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a single event that occurs during the match.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

type.description: Type of event (e.g., goal, foul, yellow, red, note).

team.description: Team associated with the event (if applicable).

time.description: Time in seconds when the event occurred.

jersey.description: Jersey number of the player involved (if applicable).

reason.description: Reason for the card or other event (if applicable).

TeamNames.title: TeamNames
TeamNames.description: Represents the names of the teams.

definition.description: Stores the complete match state, including scores, timer, events, and team names.  The document ID is 'current', indicating the active match. Each user has their own isolated match state.

params.name: appId
params.description: The ID of the application artifact.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user, obtained from Firebase Authentication.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure prioritizes authorization independence and data consistency for the Referee's Edge application. All data for a given match is stored within a single document to simplify data management and ensure atomic updates.

*   **Authorization Independence:** There are no security rules that rely on `get()` calls. Authorization is based on the user's `uid` which implicitly owns the `match_control` document. The application ensures that each user only has access to their own match data.
*   **QAPs Support:** `list` operations are secure because a user can only ever access their own `match_control` document. There is no need to filter or limit access based on content. The use of path-based ownership (`/artifacts/{appId}/users/{userId}/match_control/current`) ensures this.
